policy: MUSTER_CONDUCTOR.md 6.3 — every non-identical position probed and attributed; oracle logprobs captured with n_probs=5 in the SAME solo oracle session as the reference capture (no post-failure edits)
summary: 8-prompt committed pack at 1/5/50 greedy: 5/8 prompts token-AND-text identical at every depth (incl. code_completion, structured_json, repetitive_extraction, and the 1,867-token longctx continuation); 3 open-ended prompts flip, each at a position where the camelid token is the oracle's immediate #2 with a top-2 gap of 0.074 / 0.106 / 0.0135 nat — all far inside the 0.33-nat soft-position threshold (Ornith Q4_K_M precedent). Camelid's CPU K-quant lane emits the oracle's token at 2 of the 3 flip positions, demonstrating the flips are fp reduction-order near-ties across camelid's own backends, not decode defects.

near_ties.label: normal_chat (process vs thread)
near_ties.control: camelid CPU K-quant lane (det-run1, byte-identical across two serve sessions) emits the ORACLE token " the" at this index ("...how to use the terms...") — camelid GPU is the only backend taking the #2 branch.

near_ties.label: creative_writing (thunderstorm)
near_ties.control: camelid CPU K-quant lane emits the ORACLE token " arrival" at this index ("The storm's arrival is marked by...") — camelid's own two backends flip at exactly this position.

near_ties.label: adversarial_lowaccept
near_ties.control: camelid CPU lane took a different soft branch earlier in this sequence (index ~4), so no same-prefix CPU control exists at index 48; attribution rests on the 0.0135-nat rank-2 gap (near-coin-flip).

verdict: all three flips attributed as benign greedy near-ties; confident probes all_pass
